introduction:
In Uttar Pradesh, the Jati Praman Patra (Caste Certificate) is issued by the District Magistrate (DM) / Tehsildar / Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) for SC, ST, and OBC applicants. It is an essential document to claim reservations in education, government jobs, and welfare benefits.

UP citizens can apply online through the e-Sathi Portal (esathi.up.gov.in) or offline at the nearest Tehsil / CSC centre. The certificate is based on the ancestral permanent residential address in Uttar Pradesh.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Issued by SDM / Tehsildar of the applicant's native tehsil
  • Processing time: 15–30 working days
  • Fee: ₹10–₹20 (via e-Sathi / CSC)
  • SC/ST certificates are permanent; OBC certificates valid for 3 years
  • Online portal: e-Sathi UP (esathi.up.gov.in)
  • OBC Non-Creamy Layer income limit: ₹8 lakh per annum
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card
  • PAN Card
Caste / Nativity Proof
  • Father's Caste Certificate
  • Class X Certificate / Mark Sheet mentioning caste
  • Village land records (Khasra-Khatauni)
  • Old Ration Card with caste
Additional (OBC)
  • Income Certificate (Non-Creamy Layer)
  • Domicile / Residence Certificate
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
  • Self-declaration affidavit
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via e-Sathi UP Portal

Apply at e-Sathi UP Portal

  1. Visit Portal
    Go to esathi.up.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Register with your mobile number and Aadhaar. Verify via OTP.
  3. Select Service
    Navigate to Revenue Services → Jati Praman Patra (Caste Certificate).
  4. Fill Application
    Enter applicant name, father's name, DOB, caste, tehsil, district, and permanent address details.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of all required documents.
  6. Pay Fee
    Pay ₹10–₹20 via online payment.
  7. Submit & Track
    Submit the application and track status using your registration/application number.
  8. Download Certificate
    Download the digitally signed Jati Praman Patra from the portal once approved.
Applications can also be submitted at any Common Service Centre (CSC / Jan Seva Kendra) near you.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Tehsil / SDM Office

  1. Visit Tehsil or SDM Office
    Go to the Tehsildar / SDM office of your native tehsil in UP.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the Jati Praman Patra application form and fill all required details.
  3. Submit with Documents
    Submit the form with self-attested document copies and pay the nominal fee.
  4. Lekhpal Verification
    The Lekhpal (Revenue Inspector) of the concerned village will verify caste and residential records.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect the Jati Praman Patra from the Tehsildar office within 15–30 working days.
Processing Time: 15–30 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Track at e-Sathi Portal using your application number.

OBC Renewal

OBC certificates are valid for 3 years. Renew timely via the e-Sathi portal.

Grievance

Call 1076 (UP CM Helpline) or raise complaint at UP Jan Sunwai Portal.
